title: CertifiedSelectionReport
description: CLIENT-ATTESTED, CONTENT-FREE certified-selection report (RFC 0001 P8) sent by an SDK at session finalize when strict evidence mode (promotion_policy.require_calibration) certified a winner locally. The server CANNOT verify the certificates (their evidence never crosses the wire); it cross-checks per-CVAR coverage against the session's create-time governance and binds the winner to ITS OWN trial record — the winner's config/metrics come from the server's trial store, never from this report. NEVER carries configurations, metric values, calibrated values, value hashes, evidence counts, pool hashes, or target details: a hash of a low-entropy calibrated value is dictionary-invertible, so only the certificate's issued (freshness-context) hash crosses.

trial_id.description: The BACKEND trial_id of the certified incumbent (the id the SDK submitted results under). The server resolves the winner from its own record for this id; the trial must exist and have completed.

certificates.description: Per-CVAR certificate decisions backing the selection. Mirrors tvl_governance.certificates[] (one ontology). Server-side coverage rule: every CVAR declared governed at session create MUST appear here with decision CERTIFIED_SELECTION, or the report is rejected (fail-closed).

decision.description: Shared certificate-decision vocabulary. A certified winner requires CERTIFIED_SELECTION for every governed CVAR; the other values exist so a client can be honest about partial state, but any governed CVAR below CERTIFIED_SELECTION makes the report incomplete and the server rejects it.

freshness_hash.description: The certificate's issued hash: lowercase sha256 hex over the canonical freshness context (RFC 0001 §3.5). The context itself never crosses the wire. REQUIRED here (a certified report must bind freshness) even though tvl_governance.certificates[] keeps it optional — declaration-time entries (e.g. NO_DECISION at create) may not have one yet.

attestation.description: REQUIRED provenance marker: this report is attested by the client SDK, NOT verified by the server. v1 has exactly one value; extending the vocabulary is a schema change.
